title: Real Estate Sphere Management Software in 2026: Birthday Trackers vs Full Relationship CRMs
description: Realvolve and Wise Agent anchor this category with distinct pricing tiers. Contactually, Daylite, and LionDesk lack verifiable product pages in 2026.
tldr: Sphere management tools range from birthday-card automation to full transaction pipelines. Realvolve positions as a permanent workflow home; Wise Agent undercuts the market at $49/mo for five shared logins. Contactually, Daylite, and LionDesk yielded no active product pages in 2026—research accordingly before purchase.

body.text: Sphere-of-influence software promises to keep your past clients, referral partners, and warm leads front-of-mind without manual calendar work. The spectrum runs from lightweight birthday trackers to full-blown CRMs with transaction coordination and AI-driven task surfacing. We examined five products to surface pricing, feature trade-offs, and who wins for working agents in 2026. Three vendors returned no reachable product pages; two disclosed pricing and feature sets.

We crawled vendor pricing pages, feature lists, and public domain information. We do not fabricate testing claims or invent user counts. Where vendor pages were unreachable or pricing was unlisted, we note that explicitly. All affiliate links use the format https://[vendor-domain]/?ref=kanzenai.

No active vendor page was reachable for Contactually in our May 2026 crawl. The brand has historically offered drip campaigns and relationship buckets for real estate agents, but current pricing, feature set, and availability remain unverified. Contact the vendor directly if considering this platform.

body.name: Contactually
body.price: See vendor — no public page found
cons: No reachable product page or pricing in 2026
Cannot verify current feature set or integrations

body.text: The crawl returned a lighting-maintenance contractor in Orange County, not a CRM product. Daylite (the Mac/iOS CRM) may exist under a different domain or no longer market to real estate in 2026. We found no pricing, integration list, or feature documentation. Agents seeking Mac-native CRMs should verify current availability independently.

body.name: Daylite
body.price: See vendor — no CRM page found
pros: Historically Mac-native CRM with contact management
cons: Cannot confirm product status or pricing

body.text: No vendor page was reachable for LionDesk. The platform has historically bundled video texting, drip campaigns, and transaction management. Without current pricing or feature documentation, we cannot validate claims. Agents interested in LionDesk should request a demo and pricing sheet directly from the vendor.

body.name: LionDesk
body.price: See vendor — no public page found
pros: Historically offered video texting and transaction tools
cons: Cannot verify pricing or active integrations

body.text: Realvolve positions itself as a 'permanent home' for contacts and workflows—evolving over a career rather than churning every two years. The vendor emphasizes relationship intelligence, automated workflows, and 'login as' team oversight. Pricing is not published on the main site; agents must request a quote. The feature list includes full contact history, smart task tracking, automated workflows, and AI-assisted proactive surfacing (who to call, which deal is at risk). The platform supports email, SMS, and note capture tied to each contact. For teams, Realvolve offers clear communication logs and the ability to coach without micromanaging via login-as permissions. No specific integration list was disclosed in the crawled pages.

body.name: Realvolve
body.price: See vendor for quote — pricing not published
pros: Full contact history with notes, emails, texts in one view
AI surfaces proactive tasks and at-risk deals
'Login as' feature for team coaching without micromanaging
cons: No public pricing; must contact vendor
No published integration list or third-party connector details

body.text: Wise Agent undercuts the market at $49/mo (monthly) or $499/yr (annual, 15% savings). Both plans include up to five team members on a shared login, complimentary onboarding, and 24/7 live support. Core features: drip campaigns, lead automation, team calendar, transaction manager, customizable landing pages, unlimited training, 100 lead-enhancement credits, unlimited document storage, monthly newsletter, reporting tools, and real estate stock content. Add-ons: additional five-team-member batches ($20/mo), individual logins with permissions ($20/mo per login), unlimited lead-enhancement credits ($5/mo), WiseText with 1,000 credits/mo ($11/mo + $80 registration), extra landing pages ($5/mo per additional active page), and WiseSocial powered by Back at You ($20/mo). Enterprise plans offer bulk licensing for 15+ agents, white-label options, and custom VIP configurations. Wise Agent runs month-to-month with no contracts.

body.name: Wise Agent
body.price: $49/mo or $499/yr (up to 5 shared logins); add-ons from $5–$20/mo
pros: Flat-rate pricing with five team members included
24/7 live support and unlimited training included
Drip campaigns, transaction manager, and landing pages in base plan
cons: Shared login by default; individual logins cost $20/mo each
Texting requires $11/mo add-on plus $80 registration fee
No published integration list beyond WiseSocial partnership

items: Solo agents or small teams on a budget: Wise Agent at $49/mo delivers drip campaigns, transaction management, and five shared logins—hard to beat on price.

Teams prioritizing individual permissions and coaching: Realvolve's 'login as' feature and AI-driven task surfacing fit this use case, but request a quote to compare against Wise Agent's $20/login add-on.
Agents seeking Mac-native or video-texting legacy tools: Contactually, Daylite, and LionDesk lack verifiable 2026 product pages; verify availability independently before committing.
Any platform without a reachable product page or published pricing in 2026—three of the five tools in this round-up fall into that category.
Shared-login CRMs if your team needs granular permissions; Wise Agent's base plan uses shared credentials, requiring $20/mo per individual login.
Tools that bundle texting as a mandatory upsell if you already pay for a standalone SMS service; Wise Agent's WiseText is optional but costs $11/mo + $80 setup.
